overall.description: Gameplay footage from Red Dead Redemption 2 showing a cowboy character walking through the city of Saint Denis at night. The character approaches a grand vaudeville theater, passes through a covered alleyway beneath it, and continues through wet cobblestone streets illuminated by gas lamps toward a newspaper building.

scene_state.functional_zone: historic theater district street with wet cobblestones

agents.ego_description: walking steadily along the wet cobblestone street toward the illuminated vaudeville theater

entered.detail: ornate stone railing running along the left side of the street
grand theater building with vertical THEATRE RALEUR signage and illuminated marquee
cast iron gas lamps emitting warm orange light
multi-story brick buildings with lit windows and signage

segments.scene_description: The character walks along a wet cobblestone street at night, approaching a grand vaudeville theater illuminated by warm gas lamps. The street is empty, with a stone balustrade on the left and commercial buildings on the right.

scene_state.functional_zone: covered theater alleyway with show posters and pillars

events.description: enters covered alleyway beneath the theater marquee
exits the covered passage back to open street

agents.ego_description: walking through the narrow covered passage beneath the theater, passing show posters and pillars

entered.detail: illuminated overhead canopy with VAUDEVILLE lettering and light bulbs
green painted support columns lining the covered walkway
illuminated poster frames advertising vaudeville acts
sandwich board sign for the theater box office

segments.scene_description: The character enters a covered alleyway beneath the theater marquee, passing illuminated show posters and green support pillars. The lighting becomes darker and more enclosed before emerging back to the open street.

scene_state.functional_zone: residential city street with wooden fences

events.description: character breaks into a run briefly while traversing the open street

agents.ego_description: running briefly through the wet streets then slowing to a walk past wooden fences

entered.detail: tall wooden privacy fences lining the right side of the street
large multi-story building with illuminated signage reading Saint Denis Times

segments.scene_description: Exiting the covered passage, the character runs briefly through open cobblestone streets before slowing to a walk. Wooden fences line the right side while the Saint Denis Times building becomes visible in the distance on the left.

scene_state.functional_zone: commercial street with newspaper office entrance

agents.ego_description: walking toward the Saint Denis Times building entrance, passing a parked carriage

notable_agents.action: horse-drawn carriage parked on the street side near the building entrance

entered.detail: covered entranceway with steps leading into the Saint Denis Times building
ornate cast iron gas lamp post with warm glow

segments.scene_description: The character approaches the Saint Denis Times building, which now dominates the left side of the frame. A horse-drawn carriage is parked on the right side of the street near an ornate gas lamp post as the character walks toward the building entrance.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot follows a continuous camera movement through the nocturnal streets of Saint Denis, transitioning from the glow of a grand theater into a shadowed passage and finally toward a city newspaper office. It begins with the character walking along a wet cobblestone street illuminated by warm gas lamps, passing a stone balustrade as they approach a vaudeville theater. The scene then shifts as the camera enters a dark, enclosed alleyway beneath the theater marquee, moving past green support pillars and glowing show posters before emerging back into the open air. As the character briefly runs across the open cobblestones, the environment opens up to reveal wooden fences and the distant silhouette of the Saint Denis Times building. Finally, the camera slows as the newspaper building dominates the left side of the frame, passing a parked horse-drawn carriage and an ornate lamp post on the way to the entrance.
